behavior_summary: U8P_BRIBOOK6::func0080 launches the game's Book::read UI with a long lore string (a prose entry denying non-Titanic magic and recounting three investigated incidents), suspends until the read UI/process completes, and then records that the book was read by setting the global flag readMagBook.
This script therefore implements the player-facing 'read this book' interaction: present text, wait for the player to finish, and set persistent state so the read event can be tracked or gated.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BRIBOOK6::func0080 calls Book::read with a pointer to the book string and stores the returned process id in a local (temp).
The function issues a suspend (wait) so execution pauses until the Book::read process ends (player closes the book).
After the Book::read process completes, the function sets the global flag readMagBook = 0x01 to record the read event.
open_questions: Exact trigger: the script is inferred to run when the player interacts with the in-world book object (BRIBOOK6) but the calling/attachment point is not present in this excerpt (confidence: medium).
Downstream usage: which game systems or dialogues check readMagBook to alter behavior (quest gating, repeated prompts) is not present and remains unknown (confidence: low).
Localization: the prose is embedded directly as a long string here; whether other language/localization variants exist or are handled separately is not shown (confidence: low).

entry_conditions: Inferred: invoked when the player interacts with or chooses to read the in-world book object associated with BRIBOOK6 (likely via a use/interact handler).

external_calls.purpose: Start a separate UI/process that displays the long book text to the player. Passed a pointer/reference to the embedded string; returns a process id that is stored in a local (temp).
Pause the current execution until the Book::read process completes (wait for the player to close/finish reading).

writes.name: readMagBook
writes.likely_effect: Set to true (0x01) to record that this magic-related book has been read; likely used to avoid replaying the read interaction or to gate subsequent content/dialogue.
